data.naming_rationale: Rasa is Sanskrit for taste and essence — and the Ayurvedic model of a balanced meal (the six rasas held in balance). The mark should feel warm, appetising and calm, and quietly carry that idea of balance without being literal or spiritual-kitsch.

concepts.name: The bowl-R
concepts.description: A friendly, warm-humanist wordmark 'rasa' in sentence case (lowercase). The 'R' is drawn so its bowl reads as a rounded serving bowl — the counter of the R opens slightly and its base curve is a shade fuller, like a bowl seen from the front. A single small dot rests inside the bowl (a spice dot / a served meal / the 'tap' target). Balanced, appetising, unmistakably about food and about calm.

monogram.form: The 'R' as a bowl with one spice dot resting in it.
monogram.meaning: A served, balanced meal; the one-tap dot; the essence (rasa) held in a bowl.
monogram.construction: Built on an 8px grid; bowl curve radius = 1.5x stroke; the dot sits on the optical baseline of the bowl, diameter = 1x stroke.

wordmark.typeface_style: Custom-tuned General Sans, medium-to-semibold, all lowercase.
wordmark.special_treatment: The 'a's carry a soft double-storey warmth; the 'R' bowl is the signature.

concepts.rationale: Says 'balanced meal' and 'tap to confirm' in one soft mark, works down to a favicon as the bowl-R alone, and never reads as a diet or fitness logo.

concepts.name: The rasa drop
concepts.description: Wordmark 'rasa' beside a single teardrop/dollop mark — a warm saffron drop with a small green leaf notch, reading as a drop of essence and a hint of fresh herb. Speaks to 'taste' (a drop of flavour) and 'balanced' (warm + green in one shape).

monogram.form: Saffron teardrop with a basil-green leaf notch at the top.
monogram.meaning: A drop of taste/essence; warmth balanced by green.
monogram.construction: Golden-ratio teardrop; leaf notch cut at 30 degrees; two-colour or single-colour.

wordmark.special_treatment: Wordmark sits optically centred to the drop.

concepts.rationale: More abstract and scalable, but slightly less food-specific than the bowl-R.

concepts.name: The balance bowl
concepts.description: A minimal circular bowl mark whose rim is a single stroke and whose contents are two soft arcs — one saffron, one green — meeting level, like a balance held. Wordmark 'rasa' below or beside. The most literal 'balanced' expression.

monogram.form: Circle bowl with two level arcs (saffron over green) inside.
monogram.meaning: Balance of taste and health, held steady.
monogram.construction: Perfect circle; internal arcs meet on the horizontal centre line; equal visual weight.

concepts.rationale: Clear metaphor and a strong app-icon, but risks feeling a touch clinical/infographic versus the warmer bowl-R.

stacked.use_case: Splash screen, app store, social avatar.

monogram_only.use_case: Favicon, app icon, push-notification avatar, watermark.

clear_space.minimum: Height of the 'r' in the wordmark on all sides

app_icon.notes: Cream-on-green or saffron-on-green; rounded-square iOS/Android mask.

do: Maintain clear space equal to the 'r' height.
Use vector formats (SVG/PDF) for print, transparent PNG for digital.
Place on cream, green, or a photo only with a soft scrim behind the mark.
dont: Never stretch, skew or rotate the mark.
Never add a drop shadow, glow or bevel.
Never recolour the mark outside the palette.
Never place the warm mark on a busy food photo without a scrim.
Never capitalise the wordmark as 'RASA' or 'Rasa' in the logo (brand copy may capitalise the sentence-initial 'Rasa', but the mark stays lowercase).
Never swap the confirm-tomato into the logo — the logo is saffron/green, tomato is for the action button only.
Never outline or stroke the wordmark.
